message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: README quickstart leads with `pip install assay-engine`, but the package is not published on PyPI (404)
DIMENSION: docs vs code drift / over-claims
SEVERITY: LOW
FILE: README.md:23-26; pyproject.toml:6 README.md:24
DESCRIPTION: The README Quickstart's first instruction is `pip install assay-engine` / `pip install "assay-engine[all]"` (L24-25), presented as the primary install path with no caveat. The distribution name is assay-engine (pyproject.toml:6) but it is not on PyPI — https://pypi.org/pypi/assay-engine/json returns HTTP 404. A user copy-pasting the quickstart cannot install the package; the only working path is an editable/source install (CONTRIBUTING.md correctly uses `pip install -e ".[dev]"`). The runnable example works from source (verified: `python examples/minimal_study.py` succeeds), so the methodology demo is fine — but the headline install command is non-functional for a production/public reader. For a Beta (Development Status :: 4) package this is a real onboarding over-claim.
SUGGESTED FIX: Either publish assay-engine to PyPI, or until then add a note in the Quickstart that the package is not yet on PyPI and show the from-source install (`pip install -e .` after cloning), matching CONTRIBUTING.md. At minimum mark the pip line as the post-publication path.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# hc-assay
2	
3	**A reusable blueprint for rigorous, reproducible empirical ML/NLP on security & privacy data.**
4	
5	`hc-assay` is not a single study. It is an **engine + a methodology** that you clone onto a
6	dataset to build an independent, empirical understanding of it — and, where the dataset
7	carries external expert-asserted claims, to adjudicate those claims against that
8	independent understanding.
9	
10	Status: **engine implemented and composed.** The dataset-agnostic engine (`src/assay_engine`)
11	is in place and hardened — the contracts, the methodology core (hypotheses, three verdicts, the
12	two firewalls, content-bound pre-registration, the measurement↔interpretation fence), all five
13	infrastructure seams (tiered reasoning, self-hosted observability, persistence, orchestration,
14	baseline toolkit), an append-only hash-chained provenance trail, and a single composed runner —
15	`run_study` — that ties them into one governed end-to-end flow (ADR-0010). The engine core is
16	dependency-free; heavy backends are lazy-imported behind optional extras (`reasoning`,
17	`observability`, `persistence`, `orchestration`, `baseline`, or `all`) so it installs, imports,
18	and unit-tests offline (ADR-0006). You build a study by implementing the adapter Protocols for
19	your dataset and calling `run_study`.
20	
21	## Quickstart
22	
23	```bash
24	pip install assay-engine            # dependency-free core
25	pip install "assay-engine[all]"     # + every optional backend
26	```
27	
28	```python
29	from assay_engine import StudyPlan, run_study, auto_approve
30	
31	# implement the adapter Protocols for your dataset (parser, baseline builder, the
32	# discover/confirm callables, an optional external-claims source), then:
33	plan = StudyPlan(definition=..., source=..., baseline_builder=..., authority=..., ...)
34	
35	result = run_study(plan, gate_handler=auto_approve)   # gate_handler is required
36	print(result.phases, result.discovery_verdicts, result.scorecard)
37	result.provenance        # append-only, hash-chained audit trail (verified before return)
38	```
39	
40	A complete, runnable example (both modes, synthetic data, no backends needed) is in
41	[`examples/minimal_study.py`](examples/minimal_study.py):
42	
43	```bash
44	python examples/minimal_study.py
45	```
46	
47	`gate_handler` is required so governance is a conscious choice: pass `auto_approve` to opt out
48	of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that can block/park before each confirmatory
49	step. See [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) for the adapter contract and
50	[docs/METHODOLOGY.md](docs/METHODOLOGY.md) for the method.
51	
52	---
53	
54	## What it does
55	
56	Given a security & privacy dataset, `hc-assay`:
57	
58	1. **Builds an independent empirical baseline** of the data using ML/NLP — deterministic,
59	   versioned, and reproducible by a hostile reviewer. This baseline is the one object in
60	   the system derived purely from the data.
61	2. Lets the data **surface features, hypotheses, questions, and findings** — discovery
62	   driven by method, not by a pre-conceived narrative.
63	3. **Optionally adjudicates external expert claims.** If the dataset ships claims asserted
64	   by some external authority (relationships, labels, a taxonomy, mappings), those are
65	   converted into typed, falsifiable hypotheses and tested **against the baseline** —
66	   which is kept blind to those claims. Each test returns a verdict: **supported,
67	   contradicted, or indeterminate.**
68	4. **Scores the external source** against the validated baseline — where it aligns, where
69	   it doesn't — as an output of method, with interpretation fenced off from measurement.
70	
71	Everything runs **on-box** (local-first, data-sovereign); nothing leaves the machine.
72	
73	## Two modes
74	
75	- **Discovery** — no external claims; the data surfaces the questions and the findings.
76	- **Adjudicate external claims** — external expert assertions exist and are tested against
77	  the independent baseline. *(Optional, per dataset.)*
78	
79	The external claims are a **pluggable adapter input**. A clone may run pure discovery with
80	no claims at all. The blueprint itself never assumes any particular dataset, claim source,
81	or domain taxonomy.
82	
83	## Engine + adapter
84	
85	- The **engine** is dataset-agnostic and reusable: orchestration, governance gates, the
86	  reasoning seam, observability, persistence, the baseline toolkit, and the methodology
87	  core (hypothesis typing, three-verdict testing, the firewalls, the measurement↔
88	  interpretation fence).
89	- An **adapter** is what you write when you clone onto a new dataset: an ingestion parser,
90	  the canonical-schema binding, an optional external-claims source, a domain glossary /
91	  feature builders, and that study's pre-registration.
92	
93	Each concrete study lives in **its own repository** built on this engine, to keep every
94	study's pre-registration clean and independent.
95	
96	## Documentation
97	
98	| Doc | Purpose |
99	|---|---|
100	| [docs/CHARTER.md](docs/CHARTER.md) | Purpose, principles, scope, operating model |
101	| [docs/METHODOLOGY.md](docs/METHODOLOGY.md) | The research method, the two firewalls, verdicts, reproducibility |
102	| [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) | Engine vs adapter, components, the adapter contract, onboarding |
103	| [docs/GOVERNANCE.md](docs/GOVERNANCE.md) | Gates, pre-registration, provenance, independence, data sovereignty |
104	| [docs/GLOSSARY.md](docs/GLOSSARY.md) | Canonical terms |
105	| [docs/decisions/](docs/decisions/) | Architecture Decision Records |
106

1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["hatchling"]
3	build-backend = "hatchling.build"
4	
5	[project]
6	name = "assay-engine"
7	dynamic = ["version"]  # single-sourced from src/assay_engine/__init__.py:__version__ (#120)
8	description = "Dataset-agnostic engine for rigorous, reproducible empirical ML/NLP on security & privacy data."
9	readme = "README.md"
10	requires-python = ">=3.11,<3.15"
11	license = "Apache-2.0"
12	license-files = ["LICENSE"]
13	authors = [{ name = "thehipsterciso" }]
14	keywords = ["empirical", "reproducibility", "methodology", "security", "privacy", "ml", "nlp"]
15	classifiers = [
16	    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
17	    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
18	    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
19	    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
20	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
21	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
22	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
23	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
24	    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14",
25	    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering",
26	    "Topic :: Security",
27	    "Typing :: Typed",
28	]
29	# The engine core is dependency-free; heavy backends are optional extras (lazy-imported,
30	# ADR-0006). The composed runner and methodology work with no extras installed.
31	dependencies = []
32	
33	[project.urls]
34	Homepage = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay"
35	Repository = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay"
36	Issues = "https://github.com/thehipsterciso/hc-assay/issues"
37	
38	[project.optional-dependencies]
39	dev = [
40	    "pytest>=8",
41	    "pytest-cov>=5",  # CI integration lane runs `pytest --cov` (#103)
42	    "ruff>=0.6",
43	    "mypy>=1.11",
44	]
45	# Heavy backends for the reasoning seam — installed only where real LLM calls run.
46	# The engine imports and unit-tests (mocking the backends) without these.
47	reasoning = [
48	    "langchain-ollama>=1.1,<2",
49	    "claude-agent-sdk>=0.2.101,<0.3",
50	    "anyio>=4,<5",
51	]
52	observability = [
53	    "opentelemetry-sdk>=1.25,<2",
54	    "opentelemetry-exporter-otlp>=1.25,<2",
55	    "arize-phoenix>=7.0,<18",
56	    "openinference-instrumentation-langchain>=0.1.40,<1",
57	    "mlflow>=3.1,<4",
58	]
59	persistence = [
60	    "langgraph>=1.2,<2",  # core — provides MemorySaver (declared, not left transitive)
61	    "langgraph-checkpoint-postgres>=3.1,<4",
62	    "psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4",
63	    "qdrant-client>=1.9,<2",
64	]
65	orchestration = [
66	    "langgraph>=1.2,<2",
67	]
68	baseline = [
69	    "numpy>=1.26,<3",  # vectorized similarity at corpus scale (pure-Python fallback otherwise)
70	]
71	# Every backend in one shot (for a full local install). dev tooling stays separate.
72	all = [
73	    "assay-engine[reasoning,observability,persistence,orchestration,baseline]",
74	]
75	
76	[tool.hatch.version]
77	path = "src/assay_engine/__init__.py"
78	
79	[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
80	packages = ["src/assay_engine"]
81	
82	[tool.pytest.ini_options]
83	testpaths = ["tests"]
84	pythonpath = ["src"]
85	
86	[tool.ruff]
87	line-length = 100
88	src = ["src", "tests"]
89	
90	[tool.mypy]
91	# No fixed python_version: mypy infers the target from the running interpreter, so the CI
92	# matrix (3.11–3.14) type-checks against each version rather than only 3.11 (#121).
93	packages = ["assay_engine"]
94	strict = true
95	
96	# Optional reasoning/observability backends are not installed in the type-check env; their
97	# imports are lazy and guarded at runtime, so treat them as untyped third-party modules.
98	[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
99	module = [
100	    "langchain_ollama.*",
101	    "claude_agent_sdk.*",
102	    "anyio.*",
103	    "opentelemetry.*",
104	    "phoenix.*",
105	    "mlflow.*",
106	    "openinference.*",
107	    "langgraph.*",
108	    "qdrant_client.*",
109	    "psycopg.*",
110	    "psycopg_pool.*",
111	    "numpy.*",
112	]
113	ignore_missing_imports = true
114

1	# Contributing to hc-assay
2	
3	Thanks for your interest. hc-assay is a **dataset-agnostic engine** — the single architectural
4	rule (ADR-0002) is that the engine never imports dataset specifics. Keep that in mind for any
5	change under `src/assay_engine/`.
6	
7	## Development setup
8	
9	```bash
10	# create an environment (Python 3.11–3.14)
11	python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
12	# or: uv venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
13	
14	# dependency-free core + dev tooling
15	pip install -e ".[dev]"
16	# everything (all optional backends) for the integration tests
17	pip install -e ".[dev,all]"
18	```
19	
20	## Before you open a PR
21	
22	All three must pass (CI enforces them):
23	
24	```bash
25	ruff check . && ruff format --check src tests
26	mypy --strict src
27	pytest -q
28	```
29	
30	- The **core** lane runs with no optional backends installed (the engine must import and
31	  unit-test offline — ADR-0006). Don't add a top-level import of an optional backend; import it
32	  lazily inside the function that needs it.
33	- New behavior needs a test. Methodology/security-class code needs a *negative* test (the thing
34	  it refuses must be proven to raise).
35	- Architectural decisions go in `docs/decisions/ADR-XXXX-*.md`.
36	
37	## Project layout
38	
39	- `src/assay_engine/` — the engine (contracts, methodology, baseline, the five seams, the
40	  composed `pipeline.run_study`, provenance, registry). Dataset-agnostic.
41	- `docs/` — CHARTER, METHODOLOGY, GOVERNANCE, ARCHITECTURE, GLOSSARY, ADRs.
42	- `examples/` — a runnable, synthetic reference study.
43	- `tests/` — unit tests (core) + `tests/integration/` (need backends/services).
44

content.text: The finding is confirmed in the source: README L24-25 leads with `pip install assay-engine` / `pip install "assay-engine[all]"` with no caveat. Let me verify the package is not on PyPI and confirm the from-source path works.

input.command: curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://pypi.org/pypi/assay-engine/json 2>&1; echo " <- pypi assay-engine"; curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://pypi.org/simple/assay-engine/ 2>&1; echo " <- simple index"
input.description: Check if assay-engine is on PyPI

content.text: PyPI returns 404 for both the JSON API and the simple index — the package is genuinely unpublished. Version is 0.0.1. Let me verify the from-source example actually runs (to confirm the methodology demo works) and double-check there's no CI publish workflow that would imply it's about to be published.

content.content:
exit=0
study: example-study
phases: ['INGEST', 'BASELINE', 'DISCOVERY', 'PREREGISTER', 'CONFIRM', 'ADJUDICATE', 'SCORE', 'REPORT']
discovery verdicts: [('H1', 'supported')]
scorecard: supported=1 contradicted=0 indeterminate=0 alignment_rate=1.0
provenance entries: 20 (chain verified before return)

total 8
drwxr-xr-x  3 [REDACTED]  staff    96 Jun 16 17:32 .
drwxr-xr-x  3 [REDACTED]  staff    96 Jun 15 18:11 ..
-rw-r--r--  1 [REDACTED]  staff  3635 Jun 16 17:32 ci.yml
---grep publish/pypi/twine---
done

content.text:
All confirmed:

- **PyPI 404**: `https://pypi.org/pypi/assay-engine/json` and the simple index both return 404 — package is genuinely unpublished.
- **README leads with the broken command**: L24-25 present `pip install assay-engine` / `pip install "assay-engine[all]"` as the primary, uncaveated install path. The from-source path appears nowhere in the README Quickstart.
- **CONTRIBUTING is correct**: uses `pip install -e ".[dev]"`.
- **No publish workflow**: only `ci.yml` exists; no PyPI/twine/release automation, so publication is not imminent.
- **Example works from source**: `python examples/minimal_study.py` exits 0 and runs the full pipeline — so the methodology demo is sound; only the headline install instruction is non-functional.
- Version is `0.0.1`, but classifier is `Development Status :: 4 - Beta`.

This is a genuine docs-vs-reality drift: a public/production reader copy-pasting the first Quickstart command gets nothing installable (no `setup.py`/`-e` fallback is shown), and the README itself claims the engine "installs, imports, and unit-tests offline" (L17) reinforcing the expectation that the pip line works. It is a real onboarding defect, but low-impact: it's documentation-only, has zero runtime/security/data-integrity effect, the working path exists and is documented one file over (CONTRIBUTING.md), and the runnable example is fine. LOW severity is correctly calibrated.

I'll record this verification.

input.reasoning:
Confirmed as a genuine (low-impact) production-readiness/onboarding defect — a docs-vs-reality over-claim, not a false positive.

Verification:
1. README.md L23-26 leads the Quickstart with `pip install assay-engine` and `pip install "assay-engine[all]"` as the PRIMARY install instruction, with no caveat. The from-source/editable install does NOT appear anywhere in the README Quickstart (only `python examples/minimal_study.py` is shown, which assumes the package is already importable).
2. The distribution name is `assay-engine` (pyproject.toml:6). I confirmed via live HTTP that it is NOT on PyPI: GET https://pypi.org/pypi/assay-engine/json -> 404, and GET https://pypi.org/simple/assay-engine/ -> 404. So both headline pip commands are non-functional for any reader who copy-pastes them.
3. There is no impending publication: the only workflow is .github/workflows/ci.yml; no pypi/twine/publish/release automation exists. Version is 0.0.1 despite the `Development Status :: 4 - Beta` classifier.
4. The working path exists and is correctly documented one file over: CONTRIBUTING.md:15 uses `pip install -e ".[dev]"`. The README simply doesn't show it in the user-facing Quickstart.
5. The methodology demo itself is sound: `python examples/minimal_study.py` runs end-to-end (exit 0, all 8 phases, 20 provenance entries, chain verified). So the only broken thing is the headline install instruction.

Severity = LOW (correctly calibrated, not recalibrated): documentation-only, zero runtime/security/data-integrity/correctness impact; a working install path is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md; the runnable example works from source. It is a real onboarding/over-claim defect for a public reader (the README also claims at L17 the engine "installs, imports, and unit-tests offline," reinforcing the false expectation that the pip line works), but it cannot break a deployed system. The assessor's suggested fix (note that the package is not yet on PyPI and show the from-source install, matching CONTRIBUTING.md) is appropriate. This was not covered by pass-1 findings #101-#122 (those addressed packaging internals like single-sourced version #120, mypy matrix #121, cov dep #103 — not the README install path).
input.evidence:

```
pip install assay-engine            # dependency-free core
pip install "assay-engine[all]"     # + every optional backend
```
pyproject.toml:6 -> name = "assay-engine"; src/assay_engine/__init__.py -> __version__ = "0.0.1"; classifier "Development Status :: 4 - Beta".

Live PyPI checks:
- curl https://pypi.org/pypi/assay-engine/json -> HTTP 404
- curl https://pypi.org/simple/assay-engine/ -> HTTP 404

No publish automation: only /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml exists; grep for pypi|twine|publish over .github returned nothing.

Correct path is documented elsewhere — CONTRIBUTING.md:15: `pip install -e ".[dev]"`.

Methodology demo works from source: `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python examples/minimal_study.py` -> exit=0, phases ['INGEST','BASELINE','DISCOVERY','PREREGISTER','CONFIRM','ADJUDICATE','SCORE','REPORT'], 20 provenance entries (chain verified).

Files: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/README.md (L17, L23-26); /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/pyproject.toml (L6); /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/CONTRIBUTING.md (L15).

content.text: Confirmed TRUE. The README Quickstart (README.md:23-26) leads with `pip install assay-engine` / `pip install "assay-engine[all]"` as the primary, uncaveated install path, but the package is not on PyPI — I verified both `https://pypi.org/pypi/assay-engine/json` and the simple index return HTTP 404, and there is no publish/release workflow (only `.github/workflows/ci.yml`). The working install path (`pip install -e ".[dev]"`) exists only in CONTRIBUTING.md:15, not in the README. The runnable example works from source (exit 0, full pipeline), so only the headline install instruction is broken. LOW severity confirmed (docs-only, no runtime/security impact). Not a duplicate of pass-1 #101-#122.
